entry.title: NY Times Books
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • ‘Hollywood Ending,’ a Cradle-to-Jail Biography of Harvey Weinstein
  • Some Surprising Good News: Bookstores Are Booming and Becoming More Diverse
  • Ni Kuang, Novelist and Screenwriter for Martial Arts Films, Dies at 87
  • Review: ‘Roll Red Roll,’ by Nancy Schwartzman
  • Review: ‘The Great Man Theory,’ by Teddy Wayne
  • Book Review: “The Shores of Bohemia,” by John Taylor Williams
  • New Crime Fiction
  • Book Review: “The Crane Wife” by CJ Hauser
  • Invisibility, a Hippo and Antiques
  • Book Review: “Why Didn’t You Tell Me?” by Carmen Rita Wong and “Normal Family” by Chrysta Bilton
  • Poem: Sister Song
  • Alice Elliott Dark on ‘Fellowship Point’
  • Susie Steiner, Author of Acclaimed British Crime Novels, Dies at 51
  • With Rising Book Bans, Librarians Have Come Under Attack
  • Review: ‘Mother Noise,’ by Cindy House; ‘My Seven Black Fathers,’ by Will Jawando; and ‘This Body I Wore,’ by Diana Goetsch
  • “Our Fort” by Marie Dorléans
  • Book Review: “Tomorrow, and Tomorrow, and Tomorrow” by Gabrielle Zevin
  • Why We Need Bugs
  • Newly Published, From Banksy to Patrick Radden Keefe
  • 10 New Books We Recommend This Week
  • Book Review: “Doctors and Distillers,” by Camper English
  • How a Failed Screenwriting Career Forged a Best-Selling Author
  • Erika L. Sánchez Wishes More Authors Would Write About Money
  • Review: ‘MOTHERS DON’T,’ by Katixa Agirre
  • New Books to Read in July
The latest book reviews, best sellers, news and features from The NY TImes critics and reporters.

entry.title: London Review of Books
entry.summary: London Review of Books published at 2022-07-11 07:07AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Ferdinand Mount - Conspiracy on Cato Street: A Tale of Liberty and Revolution in Regency London
  • Letters
  • Zain Samir - Is this a new Taliban?
  • Malcolm Gaskill - Resistance: The Underground War in Europe, 1939-45
  • Jeremy Harding - In Brittany
  • Catherine Hall - Who’s Black and Why? A Hidden Chapter from the 18th-Century Invention of Race
  • Clair Wills - Either/Or
  • Adewale Maja-Pearce - Vagabonds!
  • Hal Foster - Magritte: A Life
  • Gwen Burnyeat - Short Cuts: Petro Wins
  • Rosemary Hill - France: An Adventure History
  • Paul Mendez - The Lonely Londoners
  • Francesca Wade - At Studio Voltaire
  • Blake Morrison - The Hurly Burly and Other Stories
  • Katrina Forrester - Work without the Worker: Labour in the Age of Platform Capitalism  / Dirty Work: Essential Jobs and the Hidden Toll of Inequality in America
  • Iain Sinclair - Diary: The Plutocrat Tour
Literary review publishing essay-length book reviews and topical articles on politics, literature, history, philosophy, science and the arts by leading writers and thinkers

entry.title: Five Books
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Noga Arikha on Philosophy, Science and the Body Books
  • Samuel A. Chambers on Money Books
  • Nick Enfield on Language and Post-Truth Books
  • Kevin G. Bethune on Design Books
  • Evan Lieberman on African Politics Books
  • Frank Wynne on The Best of World Literature: The 2022 International Booker Prize Shortlist
  • Myisha Cherry on Anger at Racial Injustice Books
  • Darren Collins on The Best Finance Books for Teens and Young Adults
  • Elizabeth Laird on The Best Historical Fiction: The 2022 Walter Scott Prize Shortlist
  • David George Haskell on Natural History Books
  • Gary Gerstle on Neoliberalism Books
  • Helen Scales on The Best Ocean Novels for 10-14 Year Olds
  • Jan Loeys on Market Concentration Books
  • Rosa Rankin-Gee on The Best Near-Future Dystopias
  • Cal Flyn on The Notable Novels of Summer 2022
  • Matthew Brookes & Zack Raffin on Wanderlust Books
  • Sophie Roell on Notable Nonfiction of Spring 2022
  • Carole Hillenbrand on The Best History Books: the 2022 Wolfson Prize Shortlist
  • Michele Cobb on The Best Audiobooks: the 2022 Audie Awards
  • Serhii Plokhy on Ukraine and Russia Books
  • Sophie Roell on Notable Nonfiction of Early 2022
  • Cal Flyn on Notable Novels of Spring 2022
  • Jeffrey Archer on The Best Detective Fiction
  • Marion Turner on Best Medieval Historical Fiction
Expert book recommendations

entry.title: Asian Review of Books: 10 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Asian Review of Books: 10 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-10 05:56AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • “The Emperors of Byzantium” by Kevin Lygo
  • “Chronicles of the Lost Daughters” by Debarati Mukhopadhyay
  • “Babe” by Dorothy Chan
  • Podcast with Gish Jen, author of “Thank You, Mr Nixon”
  • “The Miraculous True History of Nomi Ali” by Uzma Aslam Khan
  • “A Flutter in the Colony” by Sandeep Ray
  • “Photography in Cambodia: 1866 to the Present” by Nicholas Coffill
  • “Trinity, Trinity, Trinity” by Erika Kobayashi
  • “Amanat: Women’s Writing From Kazakhstan”, edited by Zaure Batayeva and Shelley Fairweather-Vega
  • Opera in brief: Donizetti’s “Rita” opens at the Guangzhou Opera House
In addition to reviewing books about or of relevance to Asia, the Asian Review of Books also features long-format essays by leading Asian writers and thinkers, to providing an unparalleled forum for discussion of key contemporary issues by Asians for Asia and a vehicle of intellectual depth and breadth where leading thinkers can write on the books, arts and ideas of the day. Widely quoted and referenced, with an archive of more than one thousand book reviews, it is the only web resource dedicated to Asian books. And now, with the addition of the new premium content, the Asian Review of Books, is a must-read publication.
